title: rhododendron parvifolium var. alpinum
intro: Rhododendron parvifolium var. alpinum, also known as the dwarf alpine rhododendron, is a compact, low-growing shrub native to the mountainous regions of Australia and New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves of the Rhododendron parvifolium var. alpinum contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, providing various health benefits, including protection against oxidative stress and the prevention of chronic diseases. Herbal preparations of Rhododendron parvifolium var. alpinum can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, which can be used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
